ontology_hints: standard deviation of portfolio vs index returns
relation_candidates.definition: Tracking error, also known as active risk, is a measure of the risk in an investment portfolio that is due to active management decisions made by the portfolio manager. It indicates how closely a portfolio follows the index to which it is benchmarked, and is calculated as the standard deviation of the difference between the portfolio and index returns. Tracking error excludes market‑wide movement risk and captures risk arising from stock selection, industry exposure, factor betas, and market‑timing decisions.
